original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management
original_registry_entry.version: NICE CG97; last updated 2015-06-03; last reviewed 2024-12-19
original_registry_entry.population: Men aged 18 years and over with lower urinary tract symptoms, with initial, specialist, complication, and retention qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial and specialist assessment, conservative and medicine management, referral, and urinary-retention care.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact only for adult male LUTS and urinary-retention documentation. The unrestricted legacy workflows do not encode sex or every complication qualifier; treatment, medicine choices, catheter care, surgery, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Initial assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

exact_sections.heading: Specialist assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports specialist history and examination, urinary flow, post-void residual, and recommendation-qualified imaging or cystoscopy context.

exact_sections.heading: Treating urinary retention
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Men aged 18 years and over with lower urinary tract symptoms, with initial, specialist, complication, and retention qualifiers.
intended_setting: Initial and specialist assessment, conservative and medicine management, referral, and urinary-retention care.
